zh.lifeSummary: 當Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser於1342年10月18日出生在英國林可爾史爾德尼時，他的父親Philip le Despenser29歲，母親Joan Cobham26歲。他於1362年之前與Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham在英國林可爾史爾格爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1401年8月22日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾格爾享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser于1342年10月18日出生在英国林可爾史爾德尼时，他的父亲Philip le Despenser29岁，母亲Joan Cobham26岁。他于1362年之前与Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham在英国林可爾史爾格爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1401年8月22日在英国英格兰林可爾史爾格爾享年58岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser was born on 18 October 1342, in Gedney, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Philip le Despenser, was 29 and his mother, Joan Cobham, was 26. He married Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham before 1362, in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 22 August 1401, in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser est né le 18 octobre 1342, à Gedney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Philip le Despenser, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Joan Cobham, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham avant 1362, à Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 22 août 1401, à Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser am 18. Oktober 1342 in Gedney, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Philip le Despenser 29 und seine Mutter Joan Cobham 26. Er hat vor 1362 in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 22. August 1401 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser è nato il il 18 ottobre 1342 nel Gedney, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Philip le Despenser, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Joan Cobham, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham prima del 1362, nel Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 22 agosto 1401, nel Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenserが1342年10月18日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアジェドニーで生まれた時，父Philip le Despenserは29歳，母Joan Cobhamは26歳であった。1362年以前にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアゴクスヒルでLady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1401年8月22日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアゴクスヒルで58歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser(이)가 1342년 10월 18일 영국 링코실르 게드니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philip le Despenser(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Cobham(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1362년 이전 영국 링코실르 고스힐에서 Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1401년 8월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 고스힐에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에 묻혔다.

pt.18 October 1342–22 August 1401: 18 de outubro de 1342–22 de agosto de 1401
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser nasceu em 18 de outubro de 1342, em Gedney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Philip le Despenser, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Joan Cobham, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham antes 1362, em Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de agosto de 1401, em Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser родился 18 октября 1342 г. в местности джедни, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Philip le Despenser было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Cobham, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham перед 1362 г. в местности гоксхилл, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 22 августа 1401 г. в местности гоксхилл, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.18 October 1342–22 August 1401: 18 de octubre de 1342–22 de agosto de 1401
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philip le Despenser 1st Baron le Despenser nació el 18 de octubre de 1342, en Gedney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Philip le Despenser, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Joan Cobham, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Lady Margaret (Elizabeth) de Cobham antes de 1362, en Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 22 de agosto de 1401, en Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
